converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
International Congress on the History of Sciences records
no primary creator
International Congress on the History of Sciences
14-17-744
2.6 cubic feet. 2.6 cubic feet.
1959-1964. Correspondence, abstracts, papers, periodicals, mailing lists, bulletins, minutes, memoranda, reports, tape recordings, and other material. Correspondence relating to the planning, financing, and publicity for the Congress held at Ithaca, New York, and Philadelphia in August and September, 1962. Collection material in English 2247264
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, 2008
Date modified:
RMC Staff, January 2018
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Correspondence relating to the planning, financing, and publicity for the Congress held at Ithaca, New York, and Philadelphia in August and September, 1962; also includes lists of participants, copies of abstracts and papers presented with commentaries, periodicals, and mailing lists; also, drafts and final copies of the Congress' bulletins, minutes and memoranda of the planning and local arrangement committees, reports, and other material. Also, tape recordings of the proceedings. Henry Guerlac was the president of the Congress, Richard Shryock chaired the Philadelphia operations.
Cite As:
International Congress of the History of Science records, #14-17-744. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Congresses and conventions -- Pennsylvania -- Philadelphia. Congresses and conventions -- New York (State) -- Ithaca. Science -- History. Guerlac, Henry. Shryock, Richard.
2247264 <<<<<<< HEAD Link to a finding aid.

Scope and Contents
Booklet describing Ithaca Living facilities; Program of non-academic eventsTwelve Scripts of Addresses delivered at the Congress1962-08-313
Scope and Contents
Of the twelve addresses, six deal with Symposium IV, Topic: "The Discovery of the Neutron and its Effects on Physics"; while the other six deal with general topics given throughout the congress. Unpublished guide follows.II.b. Symposium IV: Six Scripts on Topic of "The Discovery of the Neutron and its Effects Upon Physics: A case Study"date unknown

Re: History of Science CongressSeries II. Conference Papers and AddressesII.a. Tenth International Congress of the History of Science. Miscellaneous Check ListNine News Releases of Planned events by Cornell University News Bureau19623
Scope and Contents
Booklet describing Ithaca Living facilities; Program of non-academic eventsTwelve Scripts of Addresses delivered at the Congress1962-08-313
Scope and Contents
Of the twelve addresses, six deal with Symposium IV, Topic: "The Discovery of the Neutron and its Effects on Physics"; while the other six deal with general topics given throughout the congress. Unpublished guide follows.II.b. Symposium IV: Six Scripts on Topic of "The Discovery of the Neutron and its Effects Upon Physics: A case Study"date unknown
